nodes.en: Technical manuals are instructional documents detailing operation, maintenance, repair, or assembly of equipment and systems. They translate design intent into step-by-step guidance for users and technicians.
nodes.cs: Technické příručky jsou instrukční dokumenty podrobně popisující provoz, údržbu, opravu nebo montáž zařízení a systémů. Přeměňují záměr návrhu na postupný návod pro uživatele a techniky.
nodes.brief:
Technical manuals are instructional documents detailing operation, maintenance, repair, or assembly of equipment and systems. They translate design intent into step-by-step guidance for users and technicians, sitting between the engineering knowledge that produced a system and the practical skill needed to keep it running.

Within [[09]] Reference & Documentation, technical manuals draw directly on [[09.08]] Blueprints & Schematics, since a repair procedure is only actionable when paired with the diagram of what is being repaired, and they frequently cite [[09.05]] Standards & Specifications to state the tolerances and parts a technician must respect. The branch also connects to [[03]] Applied Sciences & Technology and [[07]] Practical Skills & Trades, since a manual is where engineered systems and hands-on craft meet — the document exists specifically to let a trades practitioner service equipment they did not design.

For an archive of historical and contemporary equipment manuals, see the [Internet Archive Manuals collection](https://archive.org/details/manuals), and for military technical-manual standards, the U.S. [Defense Technical Information Center](https://discover.dtic.mil/).
